title: Rip Moira Rose

jokes.text: Well they're not from me, Moira. 'Sorry for your loss. She was really something.'
jokes.setup: Johnny explains the flowers aren't from him and reads the condolence card
jokes.punchline: The card refers to Moira in past tense while she's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Strong setup for the episode's premise with good dramatic irony - flowers for a 'dead' woman being delivered to her directly

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It says 'In loving memory of Moira Rose, a light that burned too bright and is now extinguished.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's premature.

jokes.text: Ew. Who was something?
jokes.setup: Hearing she was 'really something' in a condolence message
jokes.punchline: Moira's indignant reaction to the lukewarm praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira character moment - her vanity makes her offended by faint praise even in death

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Really something? Really something?! I was a force of nature. I was a fashion icon. I was a television personality of note. I was not 'really something' — I was everything.

jokes.text: Um, well it doesn't help that Alexis just tweeted 'Goodbye to a great actress,' with a cool sunglasses emoji.
jokes.setup: Family trying to figure out why people think Moira is dead
jokes.punchline: Alexis accidentally confirmed the death rumor with a casual social media post
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - casually making everything worse while trying to be supportive

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, so people actually think mom is dead?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, I may have posted something on Instagram that could have been misinterpreted.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It was a throwback photo with a butterfly emoji and a heart. How is that a death announcement?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Because you captioned it 'Gone but never forgotten.'

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I was talking about my old highlights!

jokes.text: Ok, I'm wearing sunglasses because I'm in mourning, David.
jokes.setup: David pointing out Alexis tweeted about mourning with sunglasses emoji
jokes.punchline: Alexis literally wearing sunglasses to mourn her very-much-alive mother
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis logic - taking social media performance to absurd literal extremes

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You tweeted about mourning with a sunglasses emoji?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's called processing, David. I'm literally wearing sunglasses to mourn my very-much-alive mother.

jokes.text: so fresh, and young, and permed.
jokes.setup: Describing the 90s headshot being used for Moira's death announcement
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic addition of 'and permed' after romantic descriptors
jokes.explanation: Good comedic timing with the unexpected third descriptor that deflates the romantic tone

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It says 'She was a free spirit, wild-haired, and permed.'

jokes.text: It's not even trending, which is actually kind of sad.
jokes.setup: Discussing how widespread the death rumors are
jokes.punchline: Being more concerned about trending than the false death reports
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment - shallow priorities even in crisis situations

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not concerned about the death rumors. I'm more concerned that I'm not trending.

jokes.text: That's what you think is sad?
jokes.setup: David's concern about not trending
jokes.punchline: Johnny's incredulous reaction to David's misplaced priorities
jokes.explanation: Classic straight-man response that highlights the absurdity of the previous line

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm worried we're not going to trend.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, we're in the middle of a crisis and you're concerned about whether we're trending on Twitter?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: Who sends pink carnations?
jokes.setup: Another condolence arrangement arrives
jokes.punchline: Moira being more offended by the flower choice than the death rumors
jokes.explanation: Moira's priorities are hilariously skewed - flower aesthetics matter more than being thought dead

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, another arrangement. How thoughtful... Oh! Oh no. No, no, no. Carnations? CARNATIONS?! Does no one know that I have a standing arrangement with my florist to never, under any circumstances, allow a carnation within a fifty-foot radius of my person? I would rather be the subject of a death hoax than be associated with carnations!

jokes.text: Boy, it's strange, I can almost still smell her perfume.
jokes.setup: Roland consoling Johnny about Moira's 'death'
jokes.punchline: Claiming to smell her perfume when she just walked away moments ago
jokes.explanation: Roland's obliviousness creates good dramatic irony - he's genuinely grieving someone who just left

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, I can still smell her perfume.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: She just walked away.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: sometimes I wear Jocelyn's robe when she's outta town.
jokes.setup: Trying to relate to Johnny's 'grief'
jokes.punchline: Roland oversharing an uncomfortable personal detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland overshare that makes Johnny (and audience) uncomfortable

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, Johnny, grief is a funny thing. When my first wife left me, I didn't eat for three days. Course, that was also because I was in jail.

jokes.text: I want you to be the godfather to my child.
jokes.setup: Roland's emotional reaction to Moira's 'death'
jokes.punchline: Sudden pivot to asking Johnny to be godfather
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from grief to major life request, typical Roland boundary-crossing

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Johnny, I need you to be my kid's godfather.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, Moira just died.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I know, it's very sad. So what do you say?

jokes.text: Yeah... but are we?
jokes.setup: Roland claiming they're best friends
jokes.punchline: Johnny's hesitant questioning of their friendship status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Johnny moment - polite but honest confusion about Roland's assumption

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Are we though?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Are we really?

jokes.text: And again you know, my thing with ghosts? But she didn't seem angry like the other ones.
jokes.setup: Twyla saying she saw Moira yesterday
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning her ongoing ghost encounters and their typical anger
jokes.explanation: Excellent Twyla character moment - casually dropping supernatural experiences into normal conversation

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I saw Moira yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Yeah, she was angry. She's always angry when I see her.

jokes.text: The most projected nominee to have never actually been nominated.
jokes.setup: Reading about Moira's projected nominations
jokes.punchline: The paradox of being famous for not receiving the recognition she was expected to get
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures the absurdity of Moira's Hollywood near-misses

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, that's actually quite poetic when you think about it. Moira became famous for not receiving the recognition she was expected to get.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, well, the universe has a twisted sense of humor, doesn't it?

jokes.text: Shh. Fear not. She hath risen!
jokes.setup: Moira dramatically entering the cafe
jokes.punchline: Biblical resurrection language applied to celebrity death rumors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira grandiosity - treating tabloid rumors with Shakespearean gravitas

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I have risen from the dead. Well, not from the dead, but from a very deep sleep and several mimosas.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, you were asleep for three hours.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Exactly. I'm like Lazarus, if Lazarus had better bone structure and a skincare routine.

jokes.text: what do we think we'd be saying right now?
jokes.setup: Moira asking hypothetically what people would say if she really died
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable request for people to practice her eulogy
jokes.explanation: Moira's narcissism creates uncomfortable situation where she wants to hear her own eulogy

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I want you to practice my eulogy. I need to know how it sounds.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, that's morbid.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's not morbid, it's practical. When the time comes, I want it to be perfect. So I'm going to need all of you to write something, and then read it to me so I can provide notes.

jokes.text: Uh, she always kept things interesting. / Or uh... heaven just got its newest leading lady.
jokes.setup: Being asked to give Moira's hypothetical eulogy
jokes.punchline: Generic, lukewarm praise that barely qualifies as complimentary
jokes.explanation: The faint praise reveals how people really feel about Moira despite her self-perception

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Well, Moira was... a woman. She certainly existed. And she wore clothes. Very... memorable clothes. She was married to Johnny, which speaks to her ability to make commitments, I suppose. She had children, which means she was fertile and also willing to raise them, mostly. She will be remembered by those who knew her, probably. She did things. Many things. Some of them were intentional. She brought joy to those around her, or at least she was present while joy happened. She was alive for quite a long time. She will be missed by... some people. Possibly. In summary, Moira was a person who lived among us, and now she's gone, which is the natural order of things. Thank you.

jokes.text: Or uh... heaven just got its newest leading lady.
jokes.setup: Continuing funeral tribute exercise
jokes.punchline: Clichéd funeral speak that still centers Moira's ego
jokes.explanation: Solid follow-up that plays into Moira's theatrical self-image

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: She was a light in this world. A beacon. A... a force of nature that couldn't be contained or dimmed. Even in her final moments, she was thinking of others.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's very kind, but I'm actually still alive.

jokes.text: We make a very good team, David. You do the talking, and I sample the merchandise.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining why she needed this outing
jokes.punchline: Reducing their business partnership to David working while she eats
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis selfishness disguised as teamwork philosophy

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I needed this outing because reducing our business partnership to David working while I eat is not a partnership.

jokes.text: They were Mennonites, and I would put the peanut butter square down
jokes.setup: Alexis calling the vendors 'old farm witches'
jokes.punchline: David's correction paired with practical eating advice
jokes.explanation: David's prissy corrections and practical concerns about food pairing

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: They're not old farm witches, Alexis. They're vendors. And you should probably eat something before you insult the people providing our food.

jokes.text: Nom nom for us, David. / Never say nom nom, again.
jokes.setup: Alexis using cutesy food language
jokes.punchline: David's immediate and firm rejection of the phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - Alexis being annoying and David's pained reaction

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Can we get some nom-noms?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No. We will never use that phrase in this house.

jokes.text: She's 42, and I know!
jokes.setup: David saying Heather is like 50
jokes.punchline: Alexis correcting the age and acknowledging the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Alexis caught between defending Heather and acknowledging the age difference is significant

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Heather is like 50.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: She's 40.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: But yeah, that's weird.

jokes.text: Stop it, David, I'm not a baby.
jokes.setup: David suggesting she stay in the car with windows down
jokes.punchline: Alexis protesting while acting exactly like a child would
jokes.explanation: Alexis lacks self-awareness about her childish behavior in this exact situation

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm not a dog, David!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Then stop acting like one.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I need to come inside. I'm bored and I'm hot and my phone is dying.

jokes.text: It's not an offer, Johnny, it's an offer you can't refuse.
jokes.setup: Johnny calling the godfather role a generous offer
jokes.punchline: Roland's mangled Godfather movie reference
jokes.explanation: Roland attempting sophistication through movie quotes but getting them slightly wrong

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's a very generous offer.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, you know, I'm gonna make you an offer you can't refuse. I'm gonna be the Godfather, and you're gonna be... one of the other guys in it.

jokes.text: No, from Casino. / Okay, fine. It wasn't Casino, Brando wasn't in Casino
jokes.setup: Johnny correcting Roland's Godfather reference
jokes.punchline: Roland doubling down incorrectly then having to admit he was wrong
jokes.explanation: Roland's stubborn incorrectness followed by grudging admission creates good escalation

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's 'I'm going to make him an offer he can't refuse.' Not 'I'm going to make an offer he can't refuse.'

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm pretty sure it's 'I'm going to make an offer he can't refuse.'

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's literally one of the most famous lines in cinema history.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, maybe in YOUR cinema history. I've seen the movie plenty of times.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Have you though?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: ...No. No, I haven't. You're right. It's 'I'm going to make him an offer he can't refuse.'

jokes.text: Our house inspector said that our furnace could go any day. Oh, and I'm thinking of getting Joce his-and-hers cliff diving lessons for her birthday.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining why Johnny might need to raise their child
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dangerous and reckless lifestyle choices
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from household hazards to deliberately dangerous activities as gifts

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, if something were to happen to me, Johnny would have to raise the kid. And given my increasingly dangerous and reckless lifestyle choices, that's not out of the realm of possibility.

jokes.text: And the usual fried chicken on a waffle, extra skin.
jokes.setup: Twyla taking Roland's order
jokes.punchline: Roland's ridiculously unhealthy regular order
jokes.explanation: Establishes Roland's poor health choices right after claiming to be in good health

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: What can I get started for you today, Roland?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'll have the usual: a deep-fried butter stick, a bucket of gravy with a side of lard, and a diet cola to balance it out.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: One diet cola. You got it.

jokes.text: Roland choking and saying he's okay
jokes.setup: Johnny expressing concern about Roland's lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Roland immediately choking on his food while claiming he's fine
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy timing - Roland proves Johnny's point about his health immediately

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I'm worried about you. You need to take better care of yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm fine, I'm fine—

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: *choking violently*

jokes.text: I'll give you a bite you can't refuse.
jokes.setup: Roland offering Johnny food
jokes.punchline: Another mangled Godfather reference, callback to earlier
jokes.explanation: Weaker callback but shows Roland's persistent misuse of movie quotes

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You want some food? I got some cold cuts here.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm gonna make you an offer you can't refuse... a sandwich.

jokes.text: Do you wanna have a bite? I'll give you a bite you can't refuse.
jokes.setup: Roland offering Johnny food after the skin incident
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier Godfather reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback made more disgusting by context

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You want some food? I got some nice prosciutto.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Come on, I'm making you an offer you can't refuse.

jokes.text: Oh, I do. She is an intern.
jokes.setup: Heather thinking David has a male business partner
jokes.punchline: David quickly covering by calling Alexis an intern
jokes.explanation: David's quick thinking to avoid explaining Alexis while also diminishing her status

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: She's my intern.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm not an intern.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: She's a very new intern.

jokes.text: Fun. Mm hmm. Must be very helpful for you. How's that going?
jokes.setup: Heather mentioning she's dating a vet
jokes.punchline: Alexis awkwardly probing about Ted without revealing their connection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Alexis tries to get information while hiding her emotional investment

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: A vet? That's so... specific. What kind of vet? Like, is he... does he work with animals?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And he's... happy doing that? Like, genuinely fulfilled by his work?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Does he ever mention wanting to leave town? Or like, staying here forever? Just curious.

jokes.text: Super generic and trill.
jokes.setup: Heather saying Alexis's voice sounds familiar
jokes.punchline: Alexis describing her own voice as generic while clearly not being generic
jokes.explanation: Alexis's distinctive voice and personality make her claim of being generic ironically funny

dialogue.speaker: Heather
dialogue.line: Your voice sounds so familiar.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I know. I have such a generic voice. Like, I could be anyone.

jokes.text: Oh. No, people have told me that I just have like, one of those voices. Super generic and trill.
jokes.setup: Heather saying Alexis's voice sounds familiar
jokes.punchline: Alexis's self-deprecating deflection
jokes.explanation: Good defensive humor from Alexis in uncomfortable situation

dialogue.speaker: Heather
dialogue.line: Your voice sounds so familiar.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh, well, I mean, I've done a lot of voice work. I was the voice of a sexy lamp in a Pixar movie.

jokes.text: normally I don't like blue cheese in my mouth, but that was very... not bad.
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to give positive feedback about cheese
jokes.punchline: Her awkward phrasing and lukewarm praise
jokes.explanation: Alexis's inability to give proper compliments due to her specific food preferences

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's... cheese. And it's... here. So that's... good?

jokes.text: Get in the car!
jokes.setup: Heather mentioning her boyfriend will join them
jokes.punchline: David's panicked whisper to get Alexis out of there
jokes.explanation: David's panic at the impending disaster is perfectly economical and physical

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We need to leave. Now. Your boyfriend cannot see you like this.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Because you're covered in God knows what, and he's going to think you're insane.

jokes.text: Get in the car! Not an imposition at all.
jokes.setup: David panicking about Ted arriving
jokes.punchline: David's desperate whisper contrasted with polite response
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy moment with perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh god, oh god, he's here. What do I say? What do I do?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Just be normal. Be casual. You can do this.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Hey David!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Hi Ted. How are you?

jokes.text: Impeccably dressed woman wanders out of Podunk motel. No, that's not the headline!
jokes.setup: Moira worried about how her resurrection will be reported
jokes.punchline: Her concern about appearing to emerge from a low-class motel
jokes.explanation: Moira's vanity makes her worry about optics even in a crisis about her being thought dead

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm simply concerned about how my resurrection will be reported. The last thing I need is for the press to photograph me emerging from some dingy, low-class motel room looking like I've just been through a biblical plague.

jokes.text: This is not, not how I imagined my resurrection news to break!
jokes.setup: Moira realizing she has to face reporters
jokes.punchline: Her theatrical comparison of correcting death rumors to a resurrection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira grandiosity. Calling it a 'resurrection' shows her complete self-absorption and theatrical nature.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I have to correct the record. It's like a resurrection.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Except instead of ascending to heaven, I'm descending to a press junket.

jokes.text: It would kill Sir Tony Geary.
jokes.setup: Moira worried about disappointing her fans
jokes.punchline: Specifically naming a soap opera actor as someone who would die from disappointment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira reference - obscure soap opera knowledge delivered with complete seriousness

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I couldn't bear to disappoint my fans. They would literally die. Well, not literally. But emotionally, yes. I mean, have you seen what happened to the fans of 'The Young and the Restless' when they killed off Victor Newman? People were hospitalized.

jokes.text: But if you've ever had her quiche, you can definitely say that it is eggs-cellent.
jokes.setup: Trying to make conversation about Heather's quiche
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Bad pun but works for Alexis's character - she thinks she's being clever and charming

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So Heather's quiche... I guess you could say it's a real... *egg-cellent* choice.

jokes.text: Yes! Well, I mean, like technically I had more responsibility than just answering the phones but...
jokes.setup: Heather saying Alexis was Ted's receptionist
jokes.punchline: Alexis defensively inflating her job importance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's insecurity

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I was not his receptionist. I was his office manager. There's a big difference. I mean, I answered the phones and I scheduled appointments and I organized his files, but I was management.

jokes.text: Yes, definitely not a War Vet. Damn these fallen arches.
jokes.setup: Clarifying he's an animal vet, not war vet
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating why he's not war material with foot problems
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback that reinforces Ted's mild, non-threatening character

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I'm a veterinarian, not a war veteran.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I have flat feet and mild asthma.

jokes.text: The quiche is vivacious, and that is not a word I use to describe quiche often, but you know, when the time is right.
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to give sophisticated food commentary
jokes.punchline: Using 'vivacious' to describe quiche and explaining when she uses it for food
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Alexis trying to sound sophisticated but using words inappropriately

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: This quiche is so vivacious.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I use 'vivacious' when the food has a lot of personality, but it's still a quiche.

jokes.text: I know that David is looking for exclusivity on your products. / Ooh, she is good.
jokes.setup: Awkward lunch conversation about business
jokes.punchline: Alexis smoothly turning the situation to David's advantage and Ted's admiration
jokes.explanation: Alexis shows unexpected competence and Ted's immediate appreciation for her skills

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: So, Alexis, I heard you were thinking about starting a business venture?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, actually, it's more of a... collaborative opportunity. My brother David has this amazing eye for design and branding.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: David Rose? I didn't know he was looking to expand.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: He's not, but I think if the right project came along, something that really aligned with his aesthetic, he'd be interested. He's very selective, which is why everything he touches becomes successful.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That's... actually really impressive. I had no idea you were so business-minded.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I've learned from the best. David's taught me everything about recognizing quality and potential.

jokes.text: There was a charismatic leader, 40 something, very sexual, had very long hair, longer than you'd expect, but it worked.
jokes.setup: Moira inventing a cult story to explain their situation
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and sexual details about the fictional cult leader
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira absurdity - her theatrical mind creates unnecessarily elaborate backstory with strange details

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We were part of a very exclusive wellness collective. The leader had us all sign NDAs, so I really can't get into specifics, but let's just say he had very particular requirements about what we wore to bed, and it wasn't pajamas.

jokes.text: Truth serum. Oh Stevie, yes, those stories are always successful.
jokes.setup: Stevie suggesting telling the truth
jokes.punchline: Moira misunderstanding and adding dramatic element
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira misunderstanding that shows her dramatic mindset

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Maybe you should just tell the truth.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The truth? Darling, I haven't told the truth since I learned to accessorize. But if you insist, I shall deliver it with the gravitas of a Shakespearean soliloquy, perhaps while descending a staircase in a caftan.

jokes.text: Roland asked if you wanted to be godfather to the baby. Yes! He has been asking everybody to be the godparent. You're the 3rd person I've had to talk to today, so.
jokes.setup: Johnny thinking he's specially chosen as godfather
jokes.punchline: Roland has asked multiple people
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Johnny's presumed special status

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland asked me to be godfather. I'm very honored.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Oh, that's wonderful! Roland's so lucky to have you.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I think so too. It's quite a responsibility.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: He asked us too.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What do you mean he asked you too?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: He's asked everyone. I think he's just asking people until someone says yes.

jokes.text: Roland asked if you wanted to be godfather to the baby. / Yes! / He has been asking everybody to be the godparent. You're the 3rd person I've had to talk to today, so.
jokes.setup: Johnny excited about being chosen as godfather
jokes.punchline: Learning he's just one of many people Roland asked
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of Johnny's ego and perfect revelation of Roland's scattershot approach

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm honored to be chosen as godfather.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, actually, I asked Bob too.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: And Ronnie, and Ted, and Jocelyn.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I see.

jokes.text: Well if you like, we could um, move you up the list?
jokes.setup: Johnny learning he's not first choice for godfather
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn offering to promote him in the rankings
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that treats godparent selection like a waiting list or ranking system

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Well, you know what? I can bump you up. You could be number two.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Number two?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Yeah, I mean, Roland's always going to be number one, but you're a solid number two.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, let's face it, your first 2 were no walk in the park, right.
jokes.setup: Johnny saying his kids are good kids
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's blunt assessment of David and Alexis
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable truth delivered with Jocelyn's casual cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Well, they're not *good* kids, Johnny. They're just kids.

jokes.text: I'm just glad Ted's found someone so stable and healthy.
jokes.setup: Alexis commenting on Ted's new relationship
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing qualities she doesn't possess while clearly being hurt
jokes.explanation: Painful self-awareness from Alexis about why Ted moved on - she knows her flaws

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Ted's new girlfriend is so... smart. And kind. And she has, like, actual interests and hobbies. It's really great for him.

jokes.text: You're in love with him, aren't you? / Yes. Yup.
jokes.setup: David realizing Alexis's feelings
jokes.punchline: Alexis's simple, defeated admission
jokes.explanation: Vulnerable moment that's funny because of Alexis's uncharacteristic straightforward honesty

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, you have feelings for him?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, David. I do.

jokes.text: Um, you know that YouTube video of the giraffe and the kitten who are best friends? Okay, well the giraffe stepped on the kitten.
jokes.setup: Explaining which celebrity death replaced Moira's news
jokes.punchline: Absurd animal friendship ending in tragedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor that perfectly captures internet culture

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So a celebrity died and pushed your news story off the front page?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, and apparently it was a very famous parrot that had befriended a capybara at a zoo in São Paulo. They were inseparable for three years.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: A parrot and a capybara?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Tragically, the capybara ate the parrot.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: The capybara... ate the parrot?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Apparently they weren't as close as everyone thought.

jokes.text: Well, of course he did.
jokes.setup: Learning a giraffe stepped on its kitten friend
jokes.punchline: Moira's matter-of-fact response to the tragic news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira cynicism - treating a viral animal story with world-weary resignation

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's just nature's way of saying 'you've overstayed your welcome.'

jokes.text: you have years ahead of you still to collect a cartage of adoring mourners.
jokes.setup: Stevie saying no one would mourn her
jokes.punchline: Moira's oddly encouraging advice about accumulating future mourners
jokes.explanation: Moira's unique worldview treats mourners like a collection to be built over time

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, you're still young. You have time to make some real enemies, develop some meaningful grudges. There's still hope for you yet.

jokes.text: John, they're perishable. And excuse me, but this is the first time we've heard from most of these people. These gifts are long overdue.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting they return sympathy gifts
jokes.punchline: Moira justifying keeping gifts because people never sent them anything before
jokes.explanation: Moira's twisted logic about deserving sympathy gifts as back-payment for neglect

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not returning anything. These people have never sent me a single gift in my entire life. Now they suddenly remember I exist because we've lost everything? I think we've earned a little something.

jokes.text: 'K, Kelly Ripa just tweeted, she's taking tomorrow off. Hashtag RIP Cuppy is trending.
jokes.setup: Learning about the kitten's death
jokes.punchline: Major celebrity taking time off for internet pet death
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of absurd internet mourning culture

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A major celebrity is taking time off work because their internet cat died?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's not just any cat, David. It's an internet cat.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, I heard you the first time, but that doesn't make it any less ridiculous.

jokes.text: Honestly, what kind of kitten befriends a giraffe?
jokes.setup: Family discussing the viral video tragedy
jokes.punchline: Moira blaming the kitten for the poor friendship choice
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira victim-blaming a kitten for making poor social choices - absurdly perfect character moment

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I blame the kitten. If it hadn't been so adorable, I never would have made that poor friendship choice.

tmdb_overview: A rumor of Moira's death appears online and Roland asks Johnny to be his child's godfather.
insight: Moira's fake death generates 59 jokes in 38 minutes—character comedy sustains the absurdity.
